front.title: JSContact: Converting from and to vCard

abstract.t: This document defines how to convert contact information between the JSContact and vCard data formats. It defines conversion rules for every JSContact and vCard element registered at IANA at the time of publication. It also defines new JSContact properties as well as vCard properties and parameters, to support converting arbitrary or unknown JSContact and vCard elements. This document obsoletes RFC 9555 and updates its definitions for JSContact version "2.0".

note.t: Differences from RFC 9555 are documented in .

section.name: Notational Conventions
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.
The ABNF definitions in this document use the notations of . ABNF rules not defined in this document are defined in either (such as the ABNF for CRLF, WSP, DQUOTE, VCHAR, ALPHA, and DIGIT) or .

section.name: Scope and Goals
section.t: This document outlines how to convert between the vCard and JSContact data formats. It describes which elements are common to both, but also highlights where the two formats differ. For each common element, it defines a conversion rule and includes an example of how to convert that element. All vCard and JSContact elements currently registered at IANA are in scope, but not all of these elements are common to both formats.

For elements that have no counterpart in the other format, it is the goal of this document to define how to preserve them during conversion, but in general it is not the goal to achieve this by defining new standard elements. Instead, this document updates and by defining vCard and JSContact elements to preserve elements that have no equivalent in the other format. These conversion-specific properties are defined in Section for vCard, and for JSContact.
This document obsoletes . For contents that remain unchanged, it includes all verified errata for the obsoleted document. The conversion rules are updated for JSContact version "2.0", defined in .

replaces the JSContact "vCardProps", "vCardParams" and "vCardParam" properties originally defined in with the new "vCard" property. The new property allows for edge cases where the former elements were insufficient. For vCard, replaces the PROP-ID parameter with the new JSID parameter. Both changes align the conversion-specific elements of vCard and JSContact with those defined for iCalendar and JSCalendar in , allowing for code reuse for implementations that support these formats.

section.name: How to Read the Examples
section.t: Later sections contain examples that illustrate how to convert between the vCard and JSContact data formats. The notation of these examples is such that their main points should be clear to the reader, but their contents can also be parsed for automated testing. The following sections define the notation for such examples.

section.name: vCard Examples
section.t: A vCard example contains either an extract or a complete representation of vCard data. The following rules apply:

ul.li: An example that only contains vCard properties implicitly represents a vCard (e.g. the BEGIN:VCARD and END:VCARD lines are implicit).

The vCard implicitly contains all mandatory properties with some value. If they are not included in the example, then their actual value is irrelevant for the example. The default value for the VERSION property is "4.0".

section.t: contains three examples, all of which represent the same vCard data. In the first example, the BEGIN and END lines and the mandatory properties are implicit. In the second example, only the BEGIN and END lines of the vCard are implicit. The third example depicts a complete vCard, nothing is implicit.

figure.name: Examples for implicit and explicit vCard notation

section.t: A line containing just the value ... stands for any other properties that might be present in the vCard but are irrelevant for this example. illustrates this as an alternative representation for the examples of .

figure.name: Example for additional properties represented by ...

section.t: A line starting with a single space represents the continuation of a folded content line (). illustrates this.

figure.name: Example for a folded content line
figure.sourcecode: FN:Joannes Chrysostomus Wolfg angus Theophilus Mozart

section.name: JSContact Examples
section.t: A JSContact example always represents a Card object, even if the example only depicts other JSContact objects and properties.

JSContact objects are depicted either explicitly or implicitly. An explicit JSContact object starts and ends with braces. An implicit JSContact object omits braces, it only consists of JSON name/value pairs, separated by comma.

An implicit JSContact object is assumed to be of type Card, unless it contains the @type property with a different value. It is assumed to contain all mandatory properties with some value; if they are not depicted, their actual value is irrelevant for the main point of the example. The default version of the Card is "2.0".

illustrates this with multiple examples, all of which represent the same JSContact data. The first example contains an implicit JSContact object of type Card. The second example contains an explicit Name object but the Card object containing it is omitted. The third example contains the full Card object, nothing is omitted.

figure.name: Examples for implicit and explicit JSContact object notation

section.t: A property with name "..." and value "" stands for additional properties that might be present in a JSContact object but are irrelevant for this example, including mandatory properties. illustrates this as an alternative representation for the examples of .

figure.name: Example for additional properties represented by ...

section.name: Converting vCard to JSContact
section.t: This section contains the conversion rules from the vCard to the JSContact Card.

section.name: The Card uid Property
section.t: The UID property converts to the "uid" property of the Card object, see . In contrast to JSContact version "2.0", the Card "uid" property was mandatory in version "1.0".
Implementations that convert a vCard without a UID property to a Card of version "2.0" or higher MUST NOT generate a unique identifier as value for the uid property.

When converting a vCard without UID property to JSContact version "1.0", implementations MUST generate a value for the uid property. Generating unique identifiers is implementation-specific. An implementation SHOULD generate the same value when generating the same Card multiple times. describes why this is problematic. Consequently, implementations SHOULD NOT convert to version "1.0" Card objects.

section.name: Choosing Identifiers
section.t: Multivalued properties in JSContact are typically represented as a JSON object where the object keys are of the Id type () and the object values are the converted vCard property. In the absence of the JSID parameter (see ), implementations are free to choose any identifier as key for such entries. Whatever identifier generation scheme implementations use, they MUST generate values that are valid according to the definition of the Id type in . For example, this could be an incrementing number across all identifier keys in the Card object or only unique within one JSON object.

section.name: Converting Unknown Elements
section.t: vCards may contain elements for which no IANA-registered JSContact property is defined, such as extended properties and parameters (see ). defines the "vCard" property to represent arbitrary vCard elements in JSContact.

section.name: vCard Parameters
section.t: This section contains the conversion rules for vCard parameters. A rule typically applies only for specific vCard properties. To convert a vCard parameter on an arbitrary vCard property, see .

section.name: ALTID
section.t: The ALTID parameter () does not convert to an IANA-registered property in JSContact, but several conversion rules make use of this parameter to combine multiple vCard properties into a single JSContact object instance. For an example of this, see . To preserve the verbatim value of the ALTID parameter, set the JSContact properties defined in .

section.name: AUTHOR
section.t: The AUTHOR parameter () on a NOTE property converts to the Author object's uri property (). That Author object is set as the value of the Note object's author property ().

section.name: AUTHOR-NAME
section.t: The AUTHOR-NAME parameter () on a NOTE property converts to the Author object's name property (). That Author object is set as the value of the Note object's author property.

section.name: CALSCALE
section.t: The CALSCALE parameter () set on a BDAY, DEATHDATE, or ANNIVERSARY property converts to the PartialDate object's calendarScale property ().

section.name: CC
section.t: The CC parameter () on an ADR property converts to the Address object's countryCode property ().

section.name: CREATED
section.t: The CREATED parameter () on a NOTE property converts to the Note object's created property ().

section.name: DERIVED
section.t: The DERIVED parameter () does not convert to JSContact. If the DERIVED parameter is set to "true" on a vCard property, then implementations MAY choose not to convert that property.

section.name: GEO
section.t: The GEO parameter () set on an ADR property converts to the Address object's coordinates property (). Also see the conversion of the GEO property in .

section.name: GROUP
section.t: The GROUP parameter () does not convert to JSContact. It exclusively is for use in jCard and MUST NOT be set in a vCard.

Preserving the exact group name when converting from vCard to JSContact and back to vCard is not necessary. Any group identifiers will do, as long as the resulting vCard groups its properties equally to the original vCard. Implementations MAY preserve the original group name in the "parameters" property of the VCardProperty object ().
The following examples illustrate how to convert the GROUP parameter:

figure.name: Example of How to Preserve the Group Name for a Known Property
Example of How to Preserve the Group Name for an Unknown Property

section.name: INDEX
section.t: The INDEX parameter () set on the EXPERTISE, HOBBY, INTEREST, and ORG-DIRECTORY properties converts to the PersonalInfo () and Directory () objects' listAs property.

section.name: LANGUAGE
section.t: The LANGUAGE parameter () converts to an entry in the Card object's localizations property () for that vCard property on which this parameter is set. The value of the LANGUAGE parameter defines the language tag key in the localizations property.

This specification does not define a single standard conversion rule for how to convert the property values. Instead, building the localizations value is implementation-specific. Implementations that convert to a JSContact profile MUST adhere to the profile-specific restrictions, if any.
Two options to populate the localizations property are:

ul.li: One Patch per Property: For each vCard property with a LANGUAGE parameter, set the complete path in the PatchObject to the JSContact property that the vCard property converts to. The value of the patch is the converted property value. This is simple to process and adequate if the vCard only contains a few properties with the LANGUAGE parameter.

Bundle Patches by Parent: If a PatchObject contains multiple paths that have the same parent paths, then it might be possible to combine these patches into one patch that patches the parent property. This is possible if the property in the Card is patched in its entirety.

section.t: Generally, localizations only localize properties that are present in the non-localized version of this Card. illustrates this.
The following examples illustrate how to convert the LANGUAGE parameter:

figure.name: LANGUAGE Conversion Example: One Dominant Language

section.t: As a special case, if one or more vCard properties of the same type do not have the LANGUAGE parameter set, add them to the non-localized Card. Convert any with LANGUAGE parameters to the localizations property. illustrates this.

figure.name: LANGUAGE Conversion Example: Property without Language

section.name: LABEL
section.t: The LABEL parameter () on an ADR property converts to the Address object's full property ().

section.name: LEVEL
section.t: The LEVEL parameter () converts to the PersonalInfo object's level property (). If this parameter is set on the EXPERTISE property, then its values convert as follows:

ul.li: "beginner" converts to "low";
"average" converts to "medium"; and
"expert" converts to "high".

section.t: In all other cases, the values convert verbatim, but lowercase MUST be used for the JSContact value.

section.name: MEDIATYPE
section.t: The MEDIATYPE parameter () converts to the Resource object's mediaType property ().

section.name: PHONETIC
section.t: The PHONETIC parameter () converts to the Name () and Address () objects' phoneticSystem property unless the parameter value is "script", in which case the phoneticSystem property is not set.
The value of the SCRIPT parameter converts to the phoneticScript property (see ).
The related N or ADR property is defined by the vCard ALTID parameter. The conversion rules for the N and ADR properties define how the vCard components convert to JSContact.
The component values of the property on which the PHONETIC parameter is set, convert to the respective NameComponent or AddressComponent objects' phonetic properties.

If more than one property has the PHONETIC parameter set and relates to the same property, then they convert to the Card object's localizations property according to their LANGUAGE parameter values as outlined in .
The following example illustrates how to convert the PHONETIC parameter:

figure.name: PHONETIC Conversion Example

section.name: PID
section.t: The PID parameter () does not convert to a standard JSContact element. Implementations MAY convert it to the "parameters" property of the VCardProperty object ().

section.name: PREF
section.t: The PREF parameter () converts to the pref property of that JSContact object, to which the vCard property having this parameter converts.

section.name: PROP-ID
section.t: The PROP-ID parameter () is deprecated. The JSID parameter replaces it.

The PROP-ID parameter MUST be ignored if the JSID parameter is set on the same property. If the JSID parameter is not set, implementations MAY convert the PROP-ID parameter to the key of the JSContact object to which the vCard property converts to.
The following example illustrates how to convert the PROP-ID parameter:

section.name: SCRIPT
section.t: The SCRIPT parameter () converts to the Name () or Address () objects' phoneticScript property.
Also see .

section.name: SERVICE-TYPE
section.t: The SERVICE-TYPE parameter () converts to the OnlineService object's service property ().

section.name: SORT-AS
section.t: The SORT-AS parameter () converts to the Name, Organization, and OrgUnit objects' sortAs properties.

section.name: TYPE
section.t: The TYPE parameter () either converts to the contexts property or the kind property, as defined in later sections. If not specified otherwise, the vCard "home" and "work" parameter values convert to the JSContact "private" and "work" contexts, respectively.

section.name: TZ
section.t: The TZ parameter () on an ADR property converts to the Address object's timeZone property (). Also see the conversion of the TZ property in .

section.name: USERNAME
section.t: The USERNAME parameter () converts to the OnlineService object's user property ().

section.name: VALUE
section.t: The VALUE parameter () does not convert to an IANA-registered property in JSContact. To preserve properties with experimental values, see .

section.name: ADR
section.t: The ADR property () converts to an Address object () in the Card object's addresses property.

Each component in the structured value of the ADR property converts to an AddressComponent in the Address components property. The component value converts to the AddressComponent value property, the component position determines the value of the AddressComponent kind property as follows:

tr.td: recommends that this component not be set, but this is now disputable given the new components. Instead, set this component and use the new ADR value format defined in .

td.t: Do not convert if the ADR property value includes extended ADR components defined in .
Do not convert if the ADR property value includes extended ADR components defined in .

section.t: If the JSCOMPS parameter is set and valid, then the Address object's isOrdered property value is "true", and the defaultSeparator property and any separator name components are set according to the parameter value. The order in the components property MUST adhere to the order of the JSCOMPS parameter value.

If the JSCOMPS parameter is not set, then the Address object's isOrdered property value is "false", and the defaultSeparator property MUST NOT be set. The order in the components property MUST follow the order of values in the ADR structured value when read from left to right.

ul.li: The LABEL parameter converts to the Address object's full property.
The GEO parameter converts to the Address object's coordinates property.
The TZ parameter converts to the Address object's timeZone property.
The CC parameter converts to the Address object's countryCode property.

section.t: The ADR property converts to the same Address object as the GEO property and TZ property if either they are members of the same vCard property group, or if they are part of no group.

The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in . The ADR-specific values of the TYPE parameter defined in Sections and of convert to the corresponding entries of the contexts property as defined in .

The ALTID and LANGUAGE parameters convert according to the rules defined in . Each possible language-dependent alternative converts to an entry of the PatchObject where the key references the full property.
The path of the ADR property in the "convertedProperties" property is "addresses/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Address object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the ADR property:
See for examples of how to use the JSCOMPS parameter for structured property values.

section.name: ANNIVERSARY
section.t: The ANNIVERSARY property () converts to an Anniversary object in the Card object's anniversaries property (). The Anniversary kind is "wedding".
See how to convert property values of type TIMESTAMP and DATE.
The path of the ANNIVERSARY property in the "convertedProperties" property is "anniversaries/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Anniversary object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the ANNIVERSARY property:

section.name: BDAY
section.t: The BDAY property () converts to an Anniversary object in the Card object's anniversaries property (). Its value converts to the Anniversary date property. The Anniversary kind is "birth".

If the property value type is DATE and defines at least a year or a month and day, then the value converts to a PartialDate object. If the property value type is TIMESTAMP then the value converts to the utc property of the Timestamp object. Otherwise it does not convert to a standard JSContact element.
A BDAY property and the BIRTHPLACE property (see ) convert to the same Anniversary object if their ALTID parameter values match or are not set on either property.
The path of the BDAY property in the "convertedProperties" property is "anniversaries/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Anniversary object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the BDAY property:

figure.name: BDAY Conversion Example
figure.sourcecode: BDAY:19531015T231000Z BIRTHPLACE: 123 Main Street\nAny Town, CA 91921-1234\nU.S.A.

section.name: BIRTHPLACE
section.t: The BIRTHPLACE property () converts to an Anniversary object in the Card object's anniversaries property (). Its value converts to an Address object in the Anniversary place property. The Anniversary kind is "birth".

A property value of type TEXT converts to the Address object's full property. A property value of type URI that uses the "geo:" URI scheme converts to the Address object's coordinates property. A property having any other value does not convert to an Anniversary object.
See for an example and how to convert a BIRTHPLACE property together with a BDAY property.
The path of the BIRTHPLACE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "anniversaries/{id}/place", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Anniversary object.

section.name: CALADRURI
section.t: The CALADRURI property () converts to a SchedulingAddress object () in the Card object's schedulingAddresses property (). The SchedulingAddress object's uri property is set to the CALADRURI value.
The PREF parameter () converts according to the rules defined in .
The path of the CALADRURI property in the "convertedProperties" property is "schedulingAddresses/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the SchedulingAddress object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the CALADRURI property:

section.name: CALURI
section.t: The CALURI property () converts to a Calendar object () in the Card object's calendars property (). The Calendar object's kind property is set to "calendar" and the uri property is set to the CALURI value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the CALURI property in the "convertedProperties" property is "calendars/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Calendar object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the CALURI property:

section.name: CATEGORIES
section.t: The CATEGORIES property () converts to a set of entries of the Card object's keywords property (). The keys are the comma-separated text values of the CATEGORIES property.
In this case, the PREF parameter does not have a JSContact counterpart; however, the implementors MAY insert the entries by order of preference.

The path of the CATEGORIES property in the "convertedProperties" property is "keywords". Because more than one vCard property can convert to the keywords property, how to preserve conversion information for an individual CATEGORIES property is implementation-specific.
The following example illustrates how to convert the CATEGORIES property:

section.name: CLIENTPIDMAP
section.t: The CLIENTPIDMAP property () does not convert to a standard JSContact element. Implementations MAY convert it to the "properties" property of the VCard object ().

section.name: CONTACT-URI
section.t: The CONTACT-URI property () converts to a Link object () in the Card object's links property (). The Link object's kind property is set to "contact" and the uri property is set to the CONTACT-URI property value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the CONTACT-URI property in the "convertedProperties" property is "links/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Link object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the CONTACT-URI property:

section.name: CREATED
section.t: The CREATED property () converts to the Card object's created property ().
The path of the CREATED property in the "convertedProperties" property is "created".
The following example illustrates how to convert the CREATED property:

section.name: EMAIL
section.t: The EMAIL property () converts to an EmailAddress object () in the Card object's emails property (). The EmailAddress object's address property is set to the EMAIL value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the EMAIL property in the "convertedProperties" property is "emails/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the EmailAddress object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the EMAIL property:

section.name: DEATHDATE
section.t: The DEATHDATE property () converts to an Anniversary object in the Card object's anniversaries property (). Its value converts to the Anniversary date property. The Anniversary kind is "death".
See how to convert property values of type TIMESTAMP and DATE.
A DEATHDATE property and the DEATHPLACE property (see ) convert to the same Anniversary object if their ALTID parameter values match or are not set on either property.
The path of the DEATHDATE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "anniversaries/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Anniversary object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the DEATHDATE property:

figure.name: DEATHDATE Conversion Example
figure.sourcecode: DEATHDATE:19960415 DEATHPLACE: 5 Court Street\nNew England, ND 58647\nU.S.A.

section.name: DEATHPLACE
section.t: The DEATHPLACE property () converts to an Anniversary object in the Card object's anniversaries property (). Its value converts to an Address object in the Anniversary place property. The Anniversary kind is "death".

A property value of type TEXT converts to the Address object's full property. A property value of type URI that uses the "geo:" URI scheme converts to the Address object's coordinates property. A property having any other value does not convert to an Anniversary object.
See for an example and how to convert a DEATHPLACE property together with a DEATHDATE property.
The path of the DEATHPLACE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "anniversaries/{id}/place", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Anniversary object.

section.name: EXPERTISE
section.t: The EXPERTISE property () converts to a PersonalInfo object () in the Card object's personalInfo property (). The PersonalInfo object's kind property is set to "expertise".
The INDEX and LEVEL parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the EXPERTISE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "personalInfo/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the PersonalInfo object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the EXPERTISE property:

section.name: FBURL
section.t: The FBURL property () converts to a Calendar object () in the Card object's calendars property (). The Calendar object's kind property is set to "freeBusy" and the uri property is set to the FBURL value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the FBURL property in the "convertedProperties" property is "calendars/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Calendar object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the FBURL property:

section.name: FN
section.t: The FN property () converts to the Name object's full property (). If the LANGUAGE parameter is set, then the FN property converts as outlined in . In the unexpected case where the vCard contains more than one FN property without the LANGUAGE parameter, convert the FN property that has the least parameters. If multiple such FN properties are present, choose any of them.
All other FN properties do not convert to a standard JSContact element. Implementations MAY convert them to the "properties" property of the VCard object ().
The path of the FN property in the "convertedProperties" property is "name/full".
The following example illustrates how to convert the FN property:

section.name: GENDER
section.t: The GENDER property () does not convert to an IANA-registered property in JSContact. To convert this property, see . Alternatively, the Card object's speakToAs property defines how to address and refer to an individual represented by the Card, as do the newly defined vCard GRAMGENDER and PRONOUNS properties of .

section.name: GEO
section.t: The GEO property () converts to the Address object's coordinates property ().
See how to convert the GEO property together with the ADR and TZ property.
The path of the GEO property in the "convertedProperties" property is "addresses/{id}/coordinates", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Address object.

section.name: GRAMGENDER
section.t: The GRAMGENDER property () converts to the SpeakToAs object's grammaticalGender property ().
The path of the GRAMGENDER property in the "convertedProperties" property is "speakToAs/grammaticalGender".
The following example illustrates how to convert the GRAMGENDER property:

section.name: HOBBY
section.t: The HOBBY property () converts to a PersonalInfo object () in the Card object's personalInfo property (). The PersonalInfo object's kind property is set to "hobby".
The INDEX and LEVEL parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the HOBBY property in the "convertedProperties" property is "personalInfo/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the PersonalInfo object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the HOBBY property:

section.name: IMPP
section.t: The IMPP property () converts to an OnlineService object () in the Card object's onlineServices property (). The "uri" property is set to the IMPP value.
The SERVICE-TYPE, USERNAME, PREF, and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .

Implementations MAY use the "vCard" property to indicate that the OnlineService object was converted from the IMPP property. The path of the IMPP property in the "convertedProperties" property is "onlineServices/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the OnlineService object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the IMPP property:

section.name: INTEREST
section.t: The INTEREST property () converts to a PersonalInfo object () in the Card object's personalInfo property (). The PersonalInfo object's kind property is set to "interest".
The INDEX and LEVEL parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the INTEREST property in the "convertedProperties" property is "personalInfo/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the PersonalInfo object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the INTEREST property:

section.name: KEY
section.t: The KEY property () converts to a CryptoKey object () in the Card object's cryptoKeys property (). The CryptoKey object's uri property is set to the KEY property value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the KEY property in the "convertedProperties" property is "cryptoKeys/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the CryptoKey object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the KEY property:

section.name: KIND
section.t: The KIND property () converts to the kind property (). Allowed values are those described in and extended with the values declared in and .
The path of the KIND property in the "convertedProperties" property is "kind".
The following example illustrates how to convert the KIND property:

section.name: LANG
section.t: The LANG property () converts to a LanguagePref object () in the Card object's preferredLanguages property (). The LANG property value converts to the LanguagePref object's language property value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the LANG property in the "convertedProperties" property is "preferredLanguages/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the LanguagePref object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the LANG property:

section.name: LANGUAGE
section.t: The LANGUAGE property () converts to the Card object's language property ().
The path of the LANGUAGE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "language".
The following example illustrates how to convert the LANGUAGE property:

section.name: LOGO
section.t: The LOGO property () converts to a Media object () in the Card object's media property (). The Media object's kind property is set to "logo" and the uri property is set to the LOGO property value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the LOGO property in the "convertedProperties" property is "media/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Media object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the LOGO property:

section.name: MEMBER
section.t: The MEMBER property () converts to the Card object's members property (). Each MEMBER property value is a key in the members property.

defines the MEMBER property value type to be URI, but UID property values can either be URI or TEXT values. How to convert "member" property values that are no valid URI is implementation-specific. The PREF parameter () does not convert to JSContact.
The path of the MEMBER property in the "convertedProperties" property is "members/{value}", where "{value}" denotes the MEMBER property value.
The following example illustrates how to convert the MEMBER property:

section.name: N
section.t: The N property () converts to a Name object () in the Card object's name property.

Each component in the structured value of the N property converts to a NameComponent in the Name components property. The component value converts to the NameComponent value property, the component position determines the value of the NameComponent kind property as follows:

tr.td: Ignore any value that also occurs in the Secondary surname component.
Ignore any value that also occurs in the Generation component.

section.t: If the JSCOMPS parameter is set, then the Name object's isOrdered property value is "true", and the defaultSeparator property and any "separator" NameComponent objects are set according to the parameter value. The order in the components property MUST adhere to the order of the JSCOMPS parameter value.

If the JSCOMPS parameter is not set, then the Name object's isOrdered property value is "false", and the defaultSeparator property MUST NOT be set. The order in the components property MUST follow the order of values in the N structured value when read from left to right.

If the SORT-AS parameter is set, then its structured value converts to the Name object's sortAs property according to . An empty or non-existent component value indicates that no sort is defined for this kind.
The path of the N property in the "convertedProperties" property is "name/components".
The following example illustrates how to convert the N property:
See for examples of how to use the JSCOMPS parameter for structured property values.

section.name: NICKNAME
section.t: The NICKNAME property () converts to a Nickname object () in the Card object's nicknames property (). The name property is set to the NICKNAME property value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the NICKNAME property in the "convertedProperties" property is "nicknames/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Nickname object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the NICKNAME property:

section.name: NOTE
section.t: The NOTE property () converts to a Note object () in the Card object's notes property ().
The ALTID and LANGUAGE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the NOTE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "notes/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Note object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the NOTE property:

figure.name: NOTE Conversion Example
figure.sourcecode: NOTE;CREATED=20221123T150132Z;AUTHOR-NAME="John": Office hours are from 0800 to 1715 EST\, Mon-Fri.

section.name: ORG
section.t: The ORG property () converts to an Organization object () in the Card object's organizations property (). The Organization object's name property is set to the ORG property organizational name component. The Organization object's units property is an array of OrgUnit objects that each contain an organizational unit name component value of the ORG property value.
Implementations MAY allow representation of organizational units without the organizational name. In this case, the first component of the ORG value MUST be an empty string (e.g., ORG:;DepartmentA).
The ALTID and LANGUAGE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .

The first item of the comma-separated SORT-AS parameter value converts to the sortAs property of the Organization object. The subsequent items convert to the sortAs property of the corresponding OrgUnit object.
The TYPE parameter converts according to the rules defined in .
The path of the ORG property in the "convertedProperties" property is "organizations/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Organization object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the ORG property:

section.name: ORG-DIRECTORY
section.t: The ORG-DIRECTORY property () converts to a Directory object () in the Card object's directories property (). The Directory object's kind property is set to "directory". The uri property is set to the ORG-DIRECTORY property value.
The INDEX, PREF, and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the ORG-DIRECTORY property in the "convertedProperties" property is "directories/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Directory object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the ORG-DIRECTORY property:

section.name: PHOTO
section.t: The PHOTO property () converts to a Media object () in the Card object's media property (). The Media object's kind property is set to "photo" and the uri property is set to the PHOTO value.
The PREF and MEDIATYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the PHOTO property in the "convertedProperties" property is "media/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Media object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the PHOTO property:

section.name: PRODID
section.t: The PRODID property () converts to the Card object's prodId property ().
The path of the PRODID property in the "convertedProperties" property is "prodId".
The following example illustrates how to convert the PRODID property:

section.name: PRONOUNS
section.t: The PRONOUNS property () converts to a Pronouns object () in the pronouns property of the SpeakToAs object ().
The path of the PRONOUNS property in the "convertedProperties" property is "speakToAs/pronouns/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Pronouns object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the PRONOUNS property:

section.name: RELATED
section.t: The RELATED property () converts to the Card object's relatedTo property (). The property value converts to the key in the relatedTo property. The TYPE parameters convert to the Relation object's relation property (). Any other parameters convert as defined in .
The path of the RELATED property in the "convertedProperties" property is "relatedTo/{value}", where "{value}" denotes the RELATED property value.
The following example illustrates how to convert the RELATED property:

figure.name: RELATED Conversion Example
figure.sourcecode: RELATED;TYPE=friend:urn:uuid:f81d4fae-7dec-11d0-a765-00a0c91e6bf6 RELATED;TYPE=contact:https://example.com/directory/john.vcf RELATED;VALUE=text:Please contact my deputy John for any inquiries.

section.name: REV
section.t: The REV property () converts to the Card object's updated property ().
The path of the REV property in the "convertedProperties" property is "updated".
The following example illustrates how to convert the REV property:

section.name: ROLE
section.t: The ROLE property () converts to a Title object () in the Card object's titles property (). The Title object kind is "role". The ROLE property value converts to the Title object name property.

If the vCard contains an ORG property and either the ROLE and ORG property are not part of a vCard property group, or they are both in the same group, then the Title organizationId property is set to the identifier to which the ORG property converts in the Card object's organizations property.
The ALTID and LANGUAGE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the ROLE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "titles/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Title object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the ROLE property:

section.name: SOCIALPROFILE
section.t: The SOCIALPROFILE property () converts to an OnlineService object () in the Card object's onlineServices property (). If the SOCIALPROFILE property value is of type URI, then the "uri" property is set, otherwise, the "user" property is set.
The SERVICE-TYPE, USERNAME, PREF, and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .

Implementations MAY use the "vCard" property to indicate that the OnlineService object was converted from the SOCIALPROFILE property. The path of the SOCIALPROFILE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "onlineServices/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the OnlineService object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the SOCIALPROFILE property:

section.name: SOUND
section.t: The SOUND property () converts to a Media object () in the Card object's media property (). The Media object's kind property is set to "sound" and the uri property is set to the SOUND value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the SOUND property in the "convertedProperties" property is "media/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Media object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the SOUND property:

section.name: SOURCE
section.t: The SOURCE property () converts to a Directory object () in the Card object's directories property (). The Directory object's kind property is set to "entry". The uri property is set to the SOURCE property value.
The PREF and MEDIATYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the SOURCE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "directories/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Directory object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the SOURCE property:

section.name: TEL
section.t: The TEL property () converts to a Phone object () in the Card object's phones property ().

The TEL-specific values of the TYPE parameter convert to the features property keys as outlined in . Note that defines the default type to be "voice", but the default Phone features property is absent by default. Accordingly, an implementation SHOULD only set the Phone object's features property if the TEL property actually has a TEL-specific TYPE parameter set.
The value of the TEL property converts to the Phone object's number property.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the TEL property in the "convertedProperties" property is "phones/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Phone object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the TEL property:

section.name: TITLE
section.t: The TITLE property () converts to a Title object () in the Card object's titles property (). The TITLE property value converts to the Title object name property.
The same rules as in apply for setting the organizationId property of the Title object.
The ALTID and LANGUAGE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the TITLE property in the "convertedProperties" property is "titles/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Title object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the TITLE property:

section.name: TZ
section.t: The TZ property () converts to an Address object () in the Card object's addresses property.
A value of type TEXT converts to the Address object's timeZone property.
A value of type UTC-OFFSET converts to the Address object's timeZone property if the offset has zero minutes and the hour offset is between -12 and +14, both inclusively. Note that:

ul.li: If the hour offset is zero, use the time zone name "Etc/UTC".

Otherwise, construct the time zone name with "Etc/GMT" suffixed with the string representation of the reversed sign hour offset, including the sign but excluding leading zeros and minutes. For example, the UTC offset value "-0500" converts to "Etc/GMT+5".

section.t: See how to convert the TZ property together with the ADR and GEO property.
Any other value of type UTC-OFFSET or URI does not convert to an IANA-registered property in JSContact. To convert such property, see .
The path of the TZ property in the "convertedProperties" property is "addresses/{id}/timeZone", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Address object.

section.name: UID
section.t: The UID property () converts to the Card object's uid property ().
Implementations MAY use the "vCard" property to preserve the value type of the UID property. The path of the UID property in the "convertedProperties" property is "uid".
The following examples illustrate how to convert the UID property:

figure.name: UID Conversion Example with Value Type

section.name: URL
section.t: The URL property () converts to a Link object () in the Card object's links property (). The Link object's uri property is set to the URL value.
The PREF and TYPE parameters convert according to the rules defined in .
The path of the URL property in the "convertedProperties" property is "links/{id}", where "{id}" denotes the identifier of the Link object.
The following example illustrates how to convert the URL property:

section.name: VERSION
section.t: The VERSION property () does not convert to a standard JSContact element. Implementations MAY convert it to the "properties" property of the VCard object ().

section.name: X-ABLabel
section.t: The X-ABLabel property is experimental but widely in use in existing vCard data. It converts to the label property of a JSContact object. The X-ABLabel property is preceded by a vCard property group name, and the label converts to the JSContact object, which was converted from a vCard property of the same group.

The group name is not preserved; implementations are free to choose any unique group name when converting back to vCard. For an example on how to preserve the group name, see . The path of the X-ABLabel property in the "convertedProperties" property is "*/label", where "*" denotes the path of the object on which the label is set.
The following example illustrates how to convert the X-ABLabel property:

section.name: XML
section.t: The XML property () does not convert to a standard JSContact element. Implementations MAY convert it to the "properties" property of the VCard object ().

section.name: Converting JSContact to vCard
section.t: This section defines the conversion rules for each JSContact object type. Each subsection lists the properties of one object type and the relevant document sections for their conversion to vCard.

section.name: Address
section.t: The Address object converts to the ADR property. Each AddressComponent object in the components property converts to a component in the structured value of the ADR property.
Remarks:

ul.li: In addition to setting the "block", "direction", "district", "landmark", "name", "number", and "subdistrict" components, implementations MAY combine these values to set the "street address" component in the ADR property value.
In addition to setting the "apartment", "building", "floor" and "room" components, implementations MAY combine these values to set the "extended address" component in the ADR property value.

section.t: The other properties convert as follows:
Remarks:

ul.li: The "coordinates" and "timeZone" properties SHOULD convert to the GEO and TZ parameters, but implementations MAY alternatively convert them to the same-named properties e.g. to preserve original vCard data.

section.t: Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Anniversary
section.t: The Anniversary object converts to one of the following properties, determined by the Anniversary kind property value:

ul.li: If the kind is "wedding" and the Anniversary date property is set, then it converts to the ANNIVERSARY property. How to convert the place property is implementation-specific.
If the kind is "birth", then the date property value converts to the BDAY property and the place property converts to the BIRTHPLACE property.
If the kind is "death", then the date property value converts to the DEATHDATE property and the place property converts to the DEATHPLACE property.

section.t: A date property value of type PartialDate converts to the DATE property value type. The calendarScale property converts to the CALSCALE parameter. A date property value of type Timestamp converts to the TIMESTAMP property value type by converting the "utc" property. How to convert a place property value having any Address property other than the "full" property set is implementation-specific.
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Calendar
section.t: The Calendar object converts to one of the following properties, determined by the Calendar kind property value. If the kind is "calendar" then it converts to the CALURI property. If the kind is "freeBusy" then it converts to the FBURL property.
Its uri property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Card
section.t: The Card object converts to a vCard.
Its properties other than the localizations property convert as follows:

The localizations property converts by converting the properties in the PatchObject using the same rules as outlined in . The LANGUAGE parameter MUST be set on each resulting property with the parameter value being the language key of the localizations property. The ALTID parameter MUST be set and its value be equal for all properties having the same JSON pointer path in the localizations property and the non-localized Card.
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: CryptoKey
section.t: The CryptoKey object converts to the KEY property.
Its uri property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Directory
section.t: The Directory object converts to one of the following properties, determined by the Directory kind property value. If the kind is "entry" then it converts to the SOURCE property. If the kind is "directory" then it converts to the ORG-DIRECTORY property.
Its uri property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: EmailAddress
section.t: The EmailAddress object converts to the EMAIL property.
Its address property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: LanguagePref
section.t: The LanguagePref object converts to the LANG property.
Its language property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Link
section.t: The Link object converts to one of the following properties, determined by the Link kind property value. If the kind property is not set then it converts to the URL property.. If the kind is "contact" then it converts to the CONTACT-URI property.
Its uri property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Media
section.t: The Media object converts to one of the following properties, determined by the Media kind property value. If the kind is "photo" then it converts to the PHOTO property.. If the kind is "logo" then it converts to the LOGO property. If the kind is "sound" then it converts to the "SOUND property.
Its uri property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Name
section.t: The Name object converts to the N property. Each NameComponent object in the components property converts to a component in the structured value of the N property.
Remarks:

ul.li: In addition to converting the "generation" kind to the "Generation" component, implementations MAY also append the value to the "Honorific Suffixes" component of the N property value.
In addition to converting the "surname2" kind to the "Secondary surname" component, implementations MAY also append the value to the "Surname" component of the N property value.

section.t: The full property converts to the FN property.

The full property is optional, but the FN property is defined to be mandatory in . How to set the FN property if the full property is not set is implementation-specific. Implementations MAY derive the full name from the NameComponents objects. If the isOrdered property value is "true", then this can be done by concatenating the name component values. Alternatively, an implementation can choose any other heuristic to generate the full name from its components such as . If the FN property value is derived, implementations SHOULD set the DERIVED parameter.
The other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Nickname
section.t: The Nickname object converts to NICKNAME property.
Its name property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Note
section.t: The Note object converts to the NOTE property.
Its note property converts to the property value.
The Author object set as the value of its author property converts as follows:
Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: OnlineService
section.t: The OnlineService object converts to the IMPP property or SOCIALPROFILE property. Which property to convert to is implementation-specific. Implementations SHOULD preserve the property name indicated in the vCard property.
If its uri property is set, but neither the user or service properties are set, then implementations SHOULD choose IMPP if the uri property value is a URI with scheme "xmpp".
It other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Organization
section.t: The Organization object converts to the ORG property.

Its name property converts the first component in the structured property value. The name property of any OrgUnit object in its units property converts to additional components in the structured property value, in order to appearance.

Its sortAs property converts the first component in the structured value of the SORT-AS parameter. The sortAs property of any OrgUnit object in its units property converts to additional components in the structured parameter value, in order to appearance.
Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: PersonalInfo
section.t: The PersonalInfo object converts to one of the following properties, determined by the PersonalInfo kind property value. If the kind is "expertise" then it converts to the EXPERTISE property.. If the kind is "hobby" then it converts to the HOBBY property. If the kind is "interest" then it converts to the "INTEREST property.
Its value property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Phone
section.t: The Phone object converts to the TEL property
Its number property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: SchedulingAddress
section.t: The SchedulingAddress object converts to the CALADRURI property.
Its uri property converts to the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: SpeakToAs
section.t: The SpeakToAs object converts to the GRAMGENDER property and PRONOUNS property..
Its grammaticalGender property converts to the property value of the GRAMGENDER property.
Its pronouns property converts to PRONOUNS properties, one for each entry. The pronouns property of the Pronouns object converts the property value. Its other properties convert as follows:
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: Title
section.t: The Title object converts to one of the following properties, determined by the Title kind property value. If the kind is "title" or not set then it converts to the TITLE property.. If the kind is "role" then it converts to the ROLE property.
Its name property converts the property value. Its organizationId property converts by assigning the TITLE property and the ORG property to the same vCard property group (see ).
Other properties MAY be converted to JSPROP properties.

section.name: vCard

dl.dd: vCard
A Card object
VCard (optional)

dd.t: This property contains information about vCard data that got converted to a JSContact Card object. It allows for preserving arbitrary vCard elements, such as extension properties and parameters. This specification defines the VCard object type for converting a vCard to a JSContact Card object. Future specifications MAY define additional value types for this property.
A VCard object has the following properties:

dl.dd: This specifies the type of this object. This MUST be VCard, if set.

dd.t: This contains conversion-related information about the vCard properties that got partially or fully converted to JSContact. Each key defines the path to a JSContact element. The value for each key contains information about the vCard property which converted to the JSContact element located at that key.

Unless defined otherwise for a specific property, the path points to that JSContact element to which the vCard property converted to. For example, the path of the FN property is "name/full", but the path for a PHONE property which converted to a Phone object with key "1" is "phones/1" (not "phones/1/number"). A path MUST NOT point to or into the "localizations" property, but a localization MAY contain its own "vCard" property or patch the "vCard" property of the non-localized Card object.
The key MUST be a valid key of a PatchObject as defined in . The key MAY point into a nested object.

dl.dd: This contains properties of the vCard which did not convert to standard JSContact elements. The value MUST be a list of vCard properties formatted in jCard as defined in .

dd.t: A VCardProperty object has the following properties:

dl.dd: This specifies the type of this object. This MUST be VCardProperty, if set.
This is the name of the vCard property in lowercase.
This is the name of the vCard property value type in lowercase.
This contains parameters of the vCard property. The value MUST comply with vCard parameters formatted in jCard as defined in .

section.t: The example in describes how to convert unknown vCard elements to the "vCard" property. The "X-FOO" parameter set on the FN property illustrates how to preserve an unknown vCard parameter on a property that does convert to a standard JSContact element. The "X-BAR" property and "X-BAZ" component illustrate how to convert entirely unknown vCard properties.

figure.name: Converting unknown vCard elements to the vCard property

dl.dd: JSCOMPS
Defines the order and separators for the elements of a structured property value.

dd.t: The JSCOMPS parameter value facilitates converting name and address components between JSContact and vCard. It preserves the order of the components of the JSContact property and contains the verbatim values of separator components.

If this parameter is set and its value is valid (see later), then implementations MUST set the isOrdered property of the Name or Address object to "true". Otherwise, they MUST set the isOrdered property value to "false".

The JSCOMPS parameter value is a structured type value. Its value MUST be quoted. The parameter value consists of a sequence of entries, separated by the SEMICOLON character (U+003B). The first entry defines the value of the defaultSeparator property. If it is the empty string, then no default separator is defined. Otherwise, the first entry MUST be a separator entry. All following entries processed in order result in an ordered list of JSContact components and MUST be one of the following two kinds:

li.t: A positional. This refers to a component value in the vCard structured value. A position consists of the numeric index of a component in the structured value, optionally followed by a COMMA (U+002C) character and the non-zero index of a value within that component. The zero index selects the first component or value, respectively. The second index is zero by default, in which case it MUST be omitted (as well as the leading COMMA).

The resulting JSContact component is formed by determining its kind by the position in the vCard structured value. The component value is the verbatim value of the vCard component. Figures and illustrate this by example.

A separator. This contains the verbatim value of a separator component. It starts with the LATIN SMALL LETTER S (U+0073) character, followed by the COMMA (U+002C) character, followed by zero or more "param-value" characters (see ), where the COMMA (U+002C) and SEMICOLON (U+003B) characters MUST be escaped according to the rules defined in . illustrates this by example.
The resulting JSContact component is formed by setting its kind to "separator" and its value to the verbatim value of the entry.

dd.t: A JSCOMPS parameter value is valid if and only if:

ul.li: All indexes in the positional entries refer to an existing component value in the vCard property value.

li.t: The count of positional entries equals the count of deduplicated component values. Deduplication is required because some values may occur in both their designated and backwards-compatible components in the vCard property value:

ul.li: A value that occurs in both the N property secondary surname component and the family name component only counts once.
A value that occurs in both the N property generation component and the honorific suffix component only counts once.
A value in the ADR property street address component does not count if the ADR property value contains a value in one of the new components defined in .
All other values count once each.

dd.sourcecode:
jscomps-param = "JSCOMPS" "=" DQUOTE [jscomps-entry-sep ] ";" jscomps-entrylist DQUOTE
jscomps-entrylist = jscomps-entry *(";" jscomps-entry) jscomps-entry = jscomps-entry-pos / jscomps-entry-sep jscomps-entry-pos = 1*DIGIT [ "," 1*DIGIT ] jscomps-entry-sep = "s" "," jscomps-entry-verb jscomps-entry-verb = *QSAFE-CHAR ; encode special characters according to RFC 6868

dd.t: The following example demonstrates the use of positional entries for the name "Jane Doe". The given name is ordered before the surname. No secondary index is required for either positional because both are zero.

figure.name: Example of a Secondary Positional Index

dd.t: The following example demonstrates a secondary positional index. The "Jr." generation marker only counts once because it occurs in both the designated generation component and the backwards-compatible honorific suffixes component.

The following example demonstrates the use of separator entries for the (shortened for brevity) address "54321 Oak St, Reston". The first entry defines the default separator to be ", ". The second and fourth positional entries are separated with the separator value " ". For backwards compatibility, the street address component of the ADR property contains both the street number and name, but it is not referred to in the JSCOMPS parameter and does not contribute to the count of values.

dl.dd: JSID
Specifies the JSContact identifier of the associated property.

dd.t: This parameter specifies the JSON object key of the JSContact object to which a vCard property converts. For example, this parameter set on an EMAIL property defines the key of the EmailAddress object in the Card object's "emails" property (see below for an example).

The value of the JSID parameter MUST be a valid key according to the definition of the JSContact property to which the property that this parameter is set on converts to. Typically, this requires the value to be a valid Id.

figure.name: Converting the JSID parameter on an EMAIL property

dl.dd: JSPTR
To specify a pointer to a JSContact property.

dd.t: This parameter is set on a JSPROP property. Its value points to a JSContact property. The parameter has a single value that MUST be a valid PatchObject key as defined in . The parameter value MUST be quoted and is case-sensitive.
For further examples, see .

dl.dd: JSPROP
Represents a JSContact property in vCard.
TEXT; also see "Format definition" below for value restrictions.
Can be specified multiple times in a vCard.
The JSPTR parameter MUST be set for this property. Other IANA-registered and experimental property parameters can be specified on this property.

dd.t: This property converts an arbitrary JSContact property from and to vCard. The vCard property value is the JSON-encoded value of the JSContact property, represented as a TEXT value. The format of the JSON value MUST be compact, e.g., without insignificant whitespace as defined in . The value of the JSPTR parameter points to the JSContact property within the Card.

The root of the JSON pointer is always the Card object that this vCard converts to, irrespective if the JSON pointer starts with the SOLIDUS (U+002F) character. The pointer MUST NOT reference into an array.

All JSPROP properties in a vCard together form a PatchObject as defined in . The value of its JSPTR parameter corresponds to a key in the PatchObject; the value of the JSPROP property corresponds to the value for that key. When converting from vCard to JSContact, the PatchObject MUST only be applied after all other vCard properties have already been converted. The PatchObject MUST be valid, including the restriction that an invalid PatchObject MUST NOT be applied.
This property is defined by the following notation:
This illustrates how to convert a property at the top level in a Card object that is unknown to the implementation.

This illustrates how to convert a vendor-specific property at the top level of a Card object. Note the required use of quoted string for the JSPTR value, which allows the path to include the COLON (U+003A) character.

This illustrates how to convert a vendor-specific property at a nested level in a Card object using a path relative to the Card object. Although not recommended, the property name includes the SOLIDUS (U+002F) character, which requires escaping in the JSON pointer.

figure.name: Nested Vendor-Specific Property Example with a Path Relative to Card

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: This specification defines how to convert between the JSContact and vCard formats. The security considerations for parsing and formatting such data apply and are outlined in and .

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: TBD

front.title: JSContact: A JSON Representation of Contact Data
Unicode Locale Data Markup Language (LDML) Part 8: Person Names

section.name: Differences from RFC 9555
section.t: This section documents all significant differences from RFC 9555. Insignificant differences, such as formatting, grammar or typos are not documented.

section.name: Applied Errata
section.t: The following errrata was applied to to this document:

ul.li: Converting the UID property to the "uid" property is redefined for JSContact version "2.0". See .

section.name: Obsoleted Properties
section.t: The following JSContact properties became obsolete:

dd.t: This property is deprecated, the "properties" property of the newly defined VCard type replaces it. illustrates this using the same example as.

figure.name: Example for Representing the Obsolete vCardProps Property

dd.t: This property is deprecated, the "parameters" property of the newly defined VCardProperty type replaces it. illustrates this using the same example as .

figure.name: Example for Representing the Obsolete vCardParams Property

dd.t: This property is deprecated, the "name" property of the newly defined VCardProperty type replaces it. illustrates this using the same example as .

figure.name: Example for Representing the Obsolete vCardName Property

section.name: Obsoleted Types
section.t: The following JSContact type definitions became obsolete:

dd.t: This type is deprecated because the "vCardProps" property became deprecated. Also see .

section.name: Obsoleted Parameters
section.t: The following vCard parameters became obsolete:

dd.t: This parameter is deprecated, the newly defined JSID parameter replaces it. defines how to process the PROP-ID in presence and absence of the JSID parameter.

section.name: Acknowledgements
section.t: The definition and examples of the PHONETIC and SCRIPT parameters are based on the initial draft version of .
